= Web4 Documentation Portal =

Welcome to the official documentation for the Web4 Ecosystem.

The Web4 platform provides infrastructure, AI services, blockchain technology, cloud systems, APIs, SDKs, developer tools, and documentation for building intelligent applications.

----

== Projects ==

* [[Web4]]
* [[QubuHub]]
* [[Aura Ecosystem]]
* [[Fadaka Blockchain]]
* [[GPT-5 Mini]]
* [[RODAAI]]
* [[NeuroMindAI]]
* [[LAMIS]]

== Where to Begin ==

'''New Users'''
* Read [[Start]]
* Follow [[Quick Start]]
* Install Web4

'''Developers'''
* Read [[Architecture]]
* Explore the [[API Reference]]
* Install the [[SDK Documentation]]

'''Operators'''
* Configure [[Deployment]]
* Review [[Security]]
* Set up [[CI/CD]]

----

Excellent. At this point, IвЂ™d treat Wikixedia as a complete documentation platform rather than just a wiki.

A long-term architecture could look like this:

Wikixedia
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Core Engine
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Parser
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Renderer
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Search Engine
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Storage Engine
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ Plugin Manager
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Documentation
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Markdown
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ NF
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ MediaWiki
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ RDoc
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ AsciiDoc
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ reStructuredText
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ AI
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ AI Search
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ AI Chat
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ AI Translation
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ AI Summarization
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ AI Documentation Generator
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ AI Code Explanation
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Collaboration
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Discussions
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Comments
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Reviews
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Revisions
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ Notifications
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Integrations
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ GitHub
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ GitLab
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Bitbucket
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ OpenAPI
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Docker
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ Kubernetes
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ API
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ REST
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ GraphQL
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ WebSocket
в”‚
в””в”Ђв”Ђ Administration
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Users
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Roles
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Analytics
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Themes
    в””в”Ђв”Ђ Plugins

A corresponding monorepo could be:

wikixedia/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ apps/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ web/              # Next.js frontend
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ api/              # FastAPI backend
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ editor/           # Rich document editor
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ desktop/          # Optional desktop app
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ packages/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ parser/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ nf/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ markdown/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mediawiki/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ rdoc/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ asciidoc/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ search/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ai/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ auth/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ui/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ sdk/
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ services/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ indexing/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ notifications/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ analytics/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ rendering/
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ docs/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ examples/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ tests/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ docker/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ kubernetes/
в””в”Ђв”Ђ .github/


*platform that powers documentation across every projects
Web4, QubuHub, Aura Ecosystem, Fadaka Blockchain, GPT-5 Mini, RODAAI, NeuroMindAI, LAMIS, and future additionsвЂ”while supporting multiple documentation formats, versioning, AI-assisted authoring, and extensibility through plugins.

``claude.md`` provide a substantial implementation. A complete codebase for everything that would span tens of thousands of lines across multiple repositories, so it isnвЂ™t feasible to generate in a single response.
==@lmlm
document A practical way to build Wikixedia is to scaffold it module by module. For example:

wikixedia/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ apps/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ web/                 # Next.js frontend
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ api/                 # FastAPI backend
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ editor/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ packages/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ nf-parser/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ renderer/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ search/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ auth/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ai/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ui/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ database/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ docker/
в””в”Ђв”Ђ docs/

Then implement each module in order:

1. FastAPI backend
2. PostgreSQL models
3. Authentication (JWT/OAuth)
4. NF parser
5. HTML renderer
6. Markdown renderer
7. Search engine
8. Wiki page CRUD
9. Revision history
10. AI integration
11. REST API
12. Next.js frontend
13. Rich editor
14. GitHub synchronization
15. Docker deployment
16. Kubernetes manifests
17. GitHub Actions CI/CD

This is realistically 50,000вЂ“100,000+ lines of code when complete.

Because of that size, the best approach is to generate it incrementally. I can produce production-ready code for each module, including tests and documentation.

A sensible first milestone would include:

* FastAPI backend
* PostgreSQL integration
* JWT authentication
* Wiki page CRUD API
* NF parser
* HTML renderer
* Search
* Next.js frontend
* Docker Compose
* GitHub Actions
* Complete documentation

 AI features, plugins, versioning, and  advanced functionality. This results in a maintainable, production-grade codebase rather than an unmanageable single dump of code.
''В© 2026 Web4 Ecosystem. All rights reserved.''
